title: Module 1
content:
Deterministic approach and Probabilistic approach, Introduction to
functional safety, Overview, History and application of relevant
standards (IEC  61508,  ISO  26262,  IEC  61511),  Scope  of
IEC  61508  in Electrical/Electronic/Programmable Electronics
(E/E/PE)., Random Hardware failures, Systematic Failures, Common
cause and Common Mode failures, Safety Instrumented Function,
Safety Instrumented System, Safety Integrity Level, Systematic
Capability, Probability of Failure on Demand (PFD), Probability of
Failure per Hour (PFH), Process Safety Time (PST).

title: Module 2
content:
IEC 61511 General Principles, Risk reduction and layers of
protection, Evacuation Procedures Internal Emergency Plan (IEP),
Emergency Services External Emergency Plan (EEP), SIS Safety
life-cycle phases, Risk Assessment methods, Risk Reduction Factor,
Tolerable risk, Inductive and Deductive approaches, HAZOP, Bow
Tie Method, Prevention safeguard and Protection safeguard, Exercise
on HAZOP and Bow Tie. Allocation of Safety functions to the protection
layer, Low demand Mode, High Demand mode, Continuous Mode,
Determination of SIL for every SIF, Risk assessment Matrix, SIL
Allocation/Determination of SIF using Risk graph and LOPA Method,
Exercise on QRA.

title: Module 3
content:
SIS Safety Requirement Specifications, Independency of the
protective layers, Demand rate and operational modes, SIF structure
redundancies, Workshop reliability and acceptable level of spurious
trip. Design and realization of SIS, SIS Safety plan, Verification and
validation plan, Safety requirement specification, SIS Failure
Management, Failure types, Fail-Safe Failures, Dangerous failures,
Safe Failure Fraction, Hardware Fault Tolerance (HFT), Prior in use
requirements, Analysis and Fault detection – Basic Architecture,
Choice safety, Choice availability, Mixed choice.

title: Module 4
content:
PFD Calculation, Failure Rate of Electronic and Mechanical
components, MTTF, MTDF, MRT, MTTR, MTBF, PFD of SIF,
Reliability block diagram, Series and parallel System. Formula for
PFD and PFH, Operating or system software, Utility Software,
Application software, Software Safety lifecycle: V Cycle, Software
design Documentation, Installation and validation, Nominal Tests,
Outside Limit tests, Performance tests, Interface tests, Operation and
Maintenance of SIS, Proof tests, Impact of periodic tests, Test
Coverage/depth, Importance of test depth in PFD Calculation,
Bypass Management, Process demand, Trip test, Spurious trip,
Failures and process states, Decommissioning, Verification and
audits, Functional Safety Assessment (FSA), Independence Level
